expected.notes: seed 008-ben-inskeep: Amazon data center sanitary sewer

postText: A sanitary sewer system Amazon is constructing for 2 of its ~30 AI data center buildings at Project Rainier in New Carlisle, IN is designed to use more than 1.6 million gallons per day on hot summer days, recent IDEM filings show https://t.co/cuRyNZ41kK
keyFindings: A 224,000 SF data center facility requires significant local utility infrastructure, with an initial average sewer design flow of 17,368 GPD.
Planned data center expansion will more than triple the average sewer flow requirements to 55,000 GPD.
Cooling-related water consumption during hot weather drives extreme peak sewer flows of 1,612,000 GPD, requiring specialized regulatory approval.
The facility's design employs a peaking factor of 29.3, significantly deviating from standard technical benchmarks to accommodate seasonal climate variability.

outcome.reason: Kernel could not bind an underspecified partial
